content: He is scheduled to meet Union Minister for Tourism Gajendra Singh Shekhawat on April 2 and brief him on the progress of projects worth about ₹430 crore already under implementation

title: Tiger stepping away from golf to seek treatment
content: In a statement four days after his arrest in Florida, Tiger Woods said Tuesday he is "committed to taking the time needed to return to a healthier, stronger, and more focused place, both personally and professionally."

title: Out like a lion: Luka ends March with 600 points
content: Luka Doncic had his third straight 40-point game, lifting his March scoring total to 600 points and adding to a strong month for the Lakers, who finished with a 15-2 record.

title: Portugal outclasses U.S. amid Pulisic goal drought
content: The United States dropped its second match in a row Tuesday, losing to Portugal in a friendly at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta to conclude its March international window.

title: Goodell backs Rooney Rule amid litigation threat
content: NFL commissioner Roger Goodell says he believes the Rooney Rule is "consistent" with hiring laws after recently being threatened by Florida's attorney general with potential civil litigation if the rule was not eliminated.

title: Ump Bucknor in spotlight again after missed call
content: Umpire C.B. Bucknor, who had the poorest results among umpires in MLB's new ABS system last weekend, had another call overturned at first base during Tuesday's Rays-Brewers game.

title: No bond for amputee cornhole pro in murder case
content: Dayton Webber, the quadruple amputee cornhole player who is facing murder charges, will continue to be held without bond as his case moves forward, a Maryland judge ruled Wednesday.

title: Italy miss out on 3rd straight WC in shootout loss
content: Italy has missed out on a third consecutive World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina in a penalty shootout on Tuesday in Zenica.

title: Airfares Set To Rise As IndiGo Revises Fuel Charges For Flights
content: IndiGo on Wednesday announced that it will start levying revised fuel charges ranging from Rs 275 to Rs 10,000 on domestic and international flights from April 2, following the rise in jet fuel prices.

content: The 15-day window for self-enumeration in the New Delhi Municipal Council (NDMC) and Delhi Cantonment areas began on Wednesday.

content: Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla is the only Indian astronaut who has flown on a NASA-linked mission and trained within the American astronaut ecosystem, interacting closely with members of the Artemis II crew.

content: UIDAI has urged the EC to share data on dead voters, but no decision has been taken yet on their request, sources said.

content: A bench of Justices BV Nagarathna and Ujjal Bhuyan said the Centre's decision to pursue the matter despite the High Court granting relief reflected unnecessary litigation.

title: LIC Agent Attacked With Acid Outside Home In Andhra Pradesh
content: A shocking acid attack on an Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) agent in Andhra Pradesh's Visakhapatnam was caught on CCTV, with an unidentified man targeting the victim outside his home in Gajuwaka on Tuesday night.

content: In a shocking incident, a speeding transport bus ploughed into passengers waiting at a bus stand in Karnataka's Haveri on Wednesday morning, leaving a two-year-old child and three others injured.

title: Pushed out by Jyoti Basu, eclipsed by Mamata, crushed by BJP: Congress' 50-year exile in Bengal
content: Once a dominant force, West Bengal's Congress party has seen its influence dwindle significantly over five decades. From landslide victories to being overshadowed by communist rule and then Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress, the party now struggles for relevance.

title: Oracle layoffs: Company sets this severance condition for laid-off employees
content: Oracle has started its largest-ever layoffs, impacting thousands globally. Employees must sign termination documents via DocuSign to receive severance, which appears less generous than competitors.  These cuts, potentially reaching 30,000, aim to fund a massive AI data center expansion, a move that has placed the company under significant financial strain.

title: 'Dhurandhar2' BO Day 14: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 1000 crore net milestone
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has shattered records, officially crossing the Rs. 900 crore India net milestone. In just two weeks, the Aditya Dhar directorial has collected a staggering Rs.903.22 crore domestic net, surpassing the lifetime totals of almost every Indian blockbuster to date. Led by Ranveer Singh's career-defining performance as Hamza, the film has shown incredible resilience against the IPL 2026 matches, maintaining massive footfalls across theatres nationwide. With a current India gross of Rs. 1,081.31 crore and a global haul nearing Rs. 1,400 crore, the spy epic featuring Sanjay Dutt and Arjun Rampal is now chasing the historic Rs. 1,000 crore India net club.

title: 'No existential threat': Israeli FM jibes Iran, says can't promise this will be ‘last war’
content: Israel's foreign minister Gideon Saar cautioned that ongoing regional tensions mean the current conflict might not be the last, as adversaries still possess attack capabilities.  Despite acknowledging persistent risks, he asserted Israel isn't facing an existential threat.  Meanwhile, US President Trump claimed Iran sought a ceasefire, describing the current Iranian regime as more intelligent.

title: Watch: Rishabh Pant giggles after freak dismissal ends opening gamble in LSG vs DC
content: LSG skipper Rishabh Pant’s opening gamble backfired as he was run out for 7 in a freak dismissal during Lucknow Super Giants’ clash against Delhi Capitals. A deflection off Mukesh Kumar left him stranded. Delhi’s decision to bowl first paid off early, while Arjun Tendulkar remained absent from the squad.

title: Oracle layoffs: Employees in India, US, & other regions receive email at 6am
content: Oracle has initiated widespread global layoffs, impacting employees in the US, India, and other regions without prior notice.  Termination emails, sent directly from leadership, cited organizational changes as the reason, with affected teams seeing significant reductions.  This move is reportedly to fund Oracle's ambitious, debt-financed AI infrastructure expansion, potentially impacting thousands of jobs.

title: IndiGo revises fuel surcharge on domestic tickets from April 2
content: IndiGo is adjusting its fuel charges on domestic tickets from April 2, with new rates between Rs 275 and Rs 950, following a rise in aviation fuel prices. This comes after a previous surcharge announcement in March. While scheduled airlines see a moderate increase, non-scheduled operators face significantly higher fuel costs.

title: Salesforce CEO shares when Microsoft stopped company from investing in OpenAI
content: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff revealed Microsoft blocked their investment in OpenAI. Despite this, Salesforce secured stakes in AI firms like Cohere and Mistral, aiming to lead in enterprise AI. Benioff criticized Microsoft's competitive tactics, questioning their AI spending's impact. This highlights growing concerns over Microsoft's influence in the AI sector.

title: PCB cracks down after PSL hotel security breach, issues new 24-hour guidelines
content: The Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) has tightened security protocols for the Pakistan Super League (PSL) after a breach at a team hotel. Players now require 24-hour advance approval for family visits, with only immediate family allowed in rooms.

title: IPL: Arjun Tendulkar not even in LSG bench despite four pacers in playing XI vs DC
content: Axar Patel won the toss and chose to bowl as Delhi Capitals faced Lucknow Super Giants on a fresh pitch. Delhi fielded a balanced XI with Rahul, Miller and Kuldeep, while LSG, led by Rishabh Pant, relied on Markram, Marsh and a strong pace attack, setting up an even contest.

title: 'We'll find him another job': Rahul meets 'Yamaraj', hails UDF's health scheme
content: Rahul Gandhi encountered a man dressed as Yamaraj during his Kerala campaign, humorously suggesting the UDF's proposed Rs 25 lakh health insurance scheme would render the God of Death jobless. This initiative aims to alleviate financial burdens from medical crises, ensuring healthcare as a right for all Keralites.

title: Pakistan's Akmal makes bold claim on Sooryavanshi’s India future
content: Teenage batter Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is making headlines with explosive IPL performances, including a 35-ball century and a 15-ball fifty. Kamran Akmal believes he is ready for international cricket, praising his maturity and fearless approach. Backed by strong support, Sooryavanshi’s rapid rise has raised expectations, with Akmal saying bowlers may struggle against him in the future.

title: Watch: Woman sets SUV, two-wheelers ablaze using 'petrol bombs' near Orissa high court
content: A woman has been detained after setting fire to petrol bottles near the Orissa High Court boundary wall in Cuttack. The blaze damaged an SUV and three other vehicles parked nearby. Authorities are investigating her motive and how she obtained the petrol.

title: Fighting from hotels: Iran's missile, drone strikes force US military out of its own bases
content: Iranian attacks have severely damaged US military bases across the Middle East. American troops are now operating from temporary locations, impacting warfighting capabilities. This shift forces the Pentagon to adapt its strategy. The conflict has also affected civilian areas and critical shipping routes. The US military continues its campaign despite these challenges.

title: 'Front for Corruption': Rajnath Singh accuses LDF, UDF of 'deceiving' Kerala for decades
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh launched a strong attack on Kerala's LDF and UDF, accusing both of deceiving the state's people for decades with similar politics and governance. He alleged corruption and poor performance, stating voters are seeking a credible alternative as the BJP-led NDA intensifies its campaign for the upcoming Assembly elections.

title: Quote of the day by Julia Roberts
content: Hollywood icon Julia Roberts, celebrated for her down-to-earth charm, shares a poignant quote: "If you love someone, you say it, right then, out loud. Otherwise, the moment just passes you by.

title: Power shift? Iran military takes control of state functions, sidelines prez - report
content: Iran's IRGC has reportedly seized control of key state functions, blocking presidential appointments and decisions, pushing President Pezeshkian into a political deadlock. The IRGC chief commander intervened to reject proposed intelligence minister candidates, asserting the military's direct oversight of critical positions amid unclear Supreme Leader status.

title: 'Double-ghulami govt': Priyanka calls PM 'slave' of US, Assam CM that of Modi
content: Priyanka Gandhi accused the BJP of running a "government of double-slavery," alleging Prime Minister Modi is beholden to America and Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma is Modi's subordinate. She promised Congress welfare schemes, including financial aid for women and enhanced healthcare, while PM Modi countered by calling Congress "anti-development" and the "root cause" of corruption.

title: Prince Harry’s flirty texts to reporter surface in court during privacy trial
content: Prince Harry's legal battle against tabloids took an unexpected turn with decade-old text messages revealing a friendly rapport with a reporter. The exchanges, from 2011-2012, show a younger Harry engaging in flirtatious banter, contradicting his current testimony of limited contact and immediate disengagement upon realizing her profession. This revelation complicates his narrative of being solely a victim of the press.

title: From dead end to staging ground: How Chandrayaan-1 triggered US dash to Moon
content: Written Off As Dry & Dead After Apollo, Moon Regained Scientific & Strategic Relevance After Isro’s 2008 Mission Detected Water Molecules, Reshaping Global Space Policy, Allowing For Artemis

title: US to exit Nato? Trump slams alliance as 'paper tiger' as bloc dithers on joining Iran war
content: President Trump is strongly considering withdrawing the US from Nato, labeling the alliance a 'paper tiger' due to European allies' refusal to support US actions in the Iran conflict. He cited a lack of reciprocity and criticized the UK's stance. The US may re-examine its Nato role, with potential structural changes like a 'pay-to-play' model being discussed.

title: 'Not our war': UK PM snubs Trump, to host multi-nation meeting on reopening Hormuz
content: Prime Minister Keir Starmer affirmed Britain will not join the Middle East conflict, deeming it "not our war" and not in the UK's national interest. He announced a meeting of 35 countries to discuss reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ensuring maritime security, aiming to mitigate economic fallout and rising energy costs.

title: Arjun Tendulkar makes honest admission: 'I don't have cricketer friends'
content: Arjun Tendulkar, now with Lucknow Super Giants, shared insights into his simple personal life, saying he spends time with his dogs and has few cricketer friends. Unfazed by trolling, he said, "No, I'm not" scared of criticism. After limited chances at Mumbai Indians, he faces fresh competition but hopes to prove himself and step out of his father’s shadow.

title: 'Rs 5000 lagakar dekho': Shami on Goenka's viral boundary talks with KL Rahul, Pant
content: LSG owner Sanjiv Goenka's interactions with captains after defeats have sparked debate. However, pacer Mohammed Shami defended Goenka, stating he is passionate about cricket and his high expectations are justified due to his significant investment in the team. Shami emphasized Goenka's logical communication and positive player interactions.

title: OTP can't secure payments: RBI rolls out stricter digital payment rules
content: India's digital payments are getting a security boost from April 1 with stricter RBI authentication rules, requiring two-factor verification for all transactions to combat rising fraud.  Beyond digital payments, changes affect credit cards, FASTags, RuPay cards, PAN applications, and banking services, alongside a new income tax framework, aiming for a safer financial ecosystem.

title: ‘Ponting once again proved right’: Ashwin on Punjab Kings’ bold call
content: Ravichandran Ashwin lauded Ricky Ponting's team selection after Punjab Kings secured a narrow IPL 2026 victory against Gujarat Titans. Ashwin initially questioned the inclusion of Xavier Bartlett but was proven wrong by the player's crucial batting cameo. Cooper Connolly's debut knock anchored the chase, showcasing his composure under pressure.

title: Nifty50 tops 22,650, Sensex surges 1,187 points - top reasons for rally
content: Stock market today: Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex rallied sharply on Wednesday on hopes of an end to the US-Iran war; Nifty crossed 22,900 while Sensex surged nearly 2,000 points.

title: From income tax to FASTag fee: 10 things that will change your finances from April 1
content: Some of the changes affect credit card users, FASTag subscribers, RuPay debit cardholders. Here are some of the key revisions scheduled for implementation from the start of the new financial year.

title: Make in India boost: Armed with sensors, torpedoes, rockets, submarine hunter ‘Malwan’ joins Navy
content: The Indian Navy has received 'Malwan', its second Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft, built by Cochin Shipyard Limited. This vessel, with over 80% indigenous content, is designed for coastal defense and underwater warfare, strengthening India's maritime capabilities. The delivery aligns with the nation's push for indigenous defense manufacturing.

title: 'Hat-trick of defeat for Congress certain': PM Modi takes 'rajkumar' dig at Rahul Gandhi in Assam
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched a strong attack on Congress in Assam, dubbing Rahul Gandhi a "Rajkumar" and predicting electoral defeats. He expressed confidence in the BJP-led NDA securing a "hattrick" in the state, highlighting development under Sarbananda Sonowal and Himanta Biswa Sarma. PM Modi also promised a Uniform Civil Code and positioned Assam as a future tech hub.

title: Anti-drone laser defence system in Washington to protect Hegseth, Rubio: How it works
content: A new direct energy anti-drone system, including lasers, has been installed near Palm Beach International Airport to protect high-profile individuals and locations. This deployment addresses rising drone threats in modern warfare, adding a mobile defense layer to secure sensitive airspace and civilian aviation.

title: How a surprise call led to 'dream' St. Brown homecoming in Germany
content: amon-Ra St. Brown received a text from a team communications staffer trying to orchestrate a facetime call with his mother . his mother, Miriam, broke the news to her youngest son that the Lions would be playing a 2026 regular-season game in her home country . the full schedule of the game will be revealed later this spring .

title: UConn odds-on title favorite, 1-seeds all that remain in women's tournament
content: the women's college basketball tournament will be with -160 odds . it will be its longest since the calendar flipped to 2026 . in January, Geno Auriemma's squad was -190 to win the title . UCLA has fallen to the bottom of the odds board at +600 .

title: 2026 rankings: Profiling the top 35 QBs
content: the 2024 NFL MVP has finished in the top 10 in passing TDs six seasons in a row and remains a major threat with his legs . despite seeing less passing volume the past two seasons (14th or lower in pass attempts both years), he has finished second among quarterbacks in fantasy points . his 21.4 fantasy points per game in 2025 was actually his lowest since 2019 .

title: How a desi attorney is fighting Trump's order on birthright citizenship
content: attorney Smita Ghosh has emerged as a central intellectual architect behind the defense of the Fourteenth Amendment to the US Constitution . she is expected to play a significant role in how the justices approach a case that could redefine the meaning of US citizenship . her work is rooted in early American legal tradition .

title: Draymond Green reflects on his future amid uncertainty in Golden State

title: Chris Pronger on Auston Matthews hit, future of NHL Player Safety
content: Chris Pronger has a unique perspective on NHL player safety . he's like an inmate that eventually became a warden at the prison . Pronger was suspended for slashing, high sticking, hitting to the head, kicking and perhaps most infamously stepping on Ryan Kesler with his skate .
